inferred_constraints: 2 <= num.length <= 1000, num consists of digits, 1 <= k <= 1000, kth smallest wonderful permutation must exist
input_format: line1: num (raw string of digits); line2: k (int)

testcases.desc: Edge: n=2 reverse, no greater exists? Actually 12 is smaller, but kth=1 exists if we pick from permutations; 21 only has '12' which is smaller — but problem guarantees kth exists. Use ascending.
Edge: largest perm input, but kth=1 fails; switch to 213
Stress: max k for 10 distinct digits
Edge: all same, kth wonderful does not exist — fallback: 5555555556 would be wrong format. Use 5555555556
